docs.title: PigPig Discord Bot 🤖
docs.text: An advanced Discord AI Agent bot with modular cognition, memory, and reasoning capabilities.

docs.title: 🚀 專案特性
docs.text:
這是一個基於先進 AGI & 認知架構 設計的 Discord AI 機器人。

  • 模組化認知

    分離 Perception (感知)、Memory (記憶)、Reasoning (推理) 與 Action (行動)，避免黑箱設計。

  • 雙重記憶系統

    整合 Working Memory (工作記憶) 與 Episodic Memory (情節記憶)，讓機器人擁有「過去」與「當下」。

  • 多模型整合

    支援 Gemini 1.5 Pro/Flash, GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet 等頂尖大型語言模型。

  • 功能工廠

    具備自動化搜尋、多媒體處理、音樂播放、數學運算與系統管理等強大工具。

docs.title: 📖 文檔導覽
docs.text:
• 核心組件

    深入了解 LLM 調度、提示詞保護與資料架構。

  • 功能模組 (Cogs)

    探索從記憶指令到音樂播放的所有模組細節。

  • 管理與日誌

    配置系統設定、安全 Token 與自動更新機制。

  • 法律與政策

    隱私權政策與服務條款說明。

docs.title: 🛠️ 快速開始
docs.text:
若要開始部署您的 PigPig Bot，請參考 GitHub 上的詳細指南。

前往 GitHub 儲存庫

本計畫致力於實現機器人技術與 AI 的深度融合 (Sim2Real)。

docs.title: PigPig Discord Bot Privacy Policy
docs.text:
Effective Date: November 16, 2025 Developer: starpig1129

This Privacy Policy explains how the PigPig Discord Bot (the "Application") collects, uses, and protects your personal information when you use its features. This policy should be read in conjunction with Discord's Privacy Policy.

docs.title: 1. Information We Collect
docs.text: The Application collects the following types of data to provide its 16 core functionality modules:

docs.title: 1.1 Discord Interaction Data
docs.text:
• Basic Discord Data: Your Discord User ID, Server ID, Channel ID, message timestamps, and server membership information
  • Conversation Content: All text content of your conversations with the Application in servers and direct messages
  • Voice State Data: Voice channel join/leave events and voice state changes for music functionality

docs.title: 1.2 AI-Driven Communication Data
docs.text:
• AI Conversation Memory: The Application uses its "Episodic Memory System" to permanently store conversation history via semantic search for enhanced conversational context and memory features
  • System Prompt Data: Custom system prompt configurations and personality settings stored per server
  • Story and Narrative Data: Interactive story content, user choices, and narrative progress for the Story Management module

docs.title: 1.3 Multi-Modal Content Data
docs.text:
• Image Content: Image content you upload for Visual Question Answering (VQA), image analysis, and AI image generation/editing
  • Media Files: GIF files, multimedia content processed through various tools (GIF Tools, Image Generation)

docs.title: 1.4 Functional Preference Data
docs.text:
• User Data Management: Personal user profiles, preferences, and background information stored through the User Data system
  • Music Preferences: Playback history, queue preferences, and listening habits for music functionality
  • Channel Configuration: Server-specific settings, channel modes (whitelist/blacklist), and custom response configurations
  • Reminder and Schedule Data: Personal reminders, scheduling information, and calendar data uploaded via the Schedule Manager

docs.title: 1.5 Service Interaction Data
docs.text:
• Search Queries: Internet search history, restaurant recommendation ratings ("Eat" function), and feedback data
  • Mathematical Calculations: Mathematical expressions and calculation history for the Math Calculator module
  • Language Preferences: Server language settings and localization preferences
  • Update and System Data: Bot update logs, system status information, and operational metrics

docs.title: 1.6 Administrative Data
docs.text:
• Bot Configuration: Bot Owner ID, administrative permissions, bug report channel settings
  • Error Reports: System error logs and diagnostic information for maintenance purposes

docs.title: 2. How We Use the Information
docs.text: We collect your data solely to provide the following 16 core functionalities:

docs.title: 2.1 AI and Communication Features
docs.text:
• AI-Driven Conversations: Powered by multiple Large Language Models (LLMs) for intelligent responses
  • Intelligent Memory System: Contextual conversation recall and enhanced interaction continuity
  • Multi-Language Support: Real-time translation and localization services

docs.title: 2.2 Content Generation and Analysis
docs.text:
• Image Generation and Editing: AI-powered image creation and modification services
  • Visual Question Answering (VQA): Analysis and understanding of uploaded images
  • GIF Search and Management: Dynamic GIF searching and sharing tools

docs.title: 2.3 Entertainment and Utility Features
docs.text:
• Music Streaming: YouTube-based music playback with queue management
  • Mathematical Calculations: Advanced mathematical computation and problem-solving
  • Story and Narrative Management: Interactive storytelling and character development tools
  • Channel and Server Management: Automated channel configuration and moderation tools

docs.title: 2.4 Productivity and Organization
docs.text:
• Reminder System: Personal and group reminder management
  • Schedule Management: Calendar integration and event scheduling
  • Conversation Summarization: AI-powered conversation analysis and summarization
  • Internet Search: Integrated web search and research capabilities

docs.title: 2.5 System and Maintenance
docs.text:
• Automated Updates: Self-maintaining update system from GitHub repositories
  • System Prompt Management: Customizable bot personality and behavior settings
  • User Data Management: Personalized user profiles and preference learning

docs.title: 3. Data Sharing and Storage (Third-Party Services)
docs.text: As the Application is driven by multiple Large Language Models (LLMs) and external services, your interaction data will be shared with third-party service providers:

docs.title: 3.1 AI and Language Model Services
docs.text:
To provide AI responses and multi-modal functions, your conversation content, image content, voice data, and interaction metadata will be transmitted to the following Third-Party API Services for processing:

  • Anthropic (Claude): For advanced conversational AI and reasoning capabilities
  • OpenAI (GPT): For language understanding, generation, and analysis
  • Google (Gemini): For multi-modal AI, image analysis, and grounding capabilities
  • Local AI Models: For privacy-sensitive operations and offline functionality

Important: These services have their own independent Terms of Service and Privacy Policies. You should review their respective policies.

docs.title: 3.2 Content and Media Services
docs.text:
• YouTube API: For music streaming, search, and metadata retrieval
  • Google Search API: For internet search functionality
  • GIF Services: For GIF search and retrieval operations

docs.title: 3.3 Data Storage and Processing
docs.text:
• Primary Storage: Data is stored on Virtual Private Servers (VPS) with appropriate security measures
  • Backup Systems: Regular backups maintained for data protection and recovery
  • Data Location: Server infrastructure may be located in various jurisdictions for optimal performance

docs.title: 4.1 Data Retention Policy
docs.text:
• Long-Term Memory: Your conversation memory data is retained indefinitely until actively deleted by you or a server administrator using bot commands
  • User Data: Personal user information is maintained as long as the Application remains active in your server
  • Temporary Data: Search queries, calculations, and temporary processing data are typically retained for 30 days unless needed for troubleshooting
  • Deletion Triggers: Data is automatically deleted when the Application is removed from the server or through explicit user commands

docs.title: 4.2 Your Rights
docs.text: You have the right to: - Access: Request access to personal data stored by the Application - Correction: Request correction of inaccurate personal information - Deletion: Request deletion of your personal data - Portability: Request a copy of your data in a structured format - Restriction: Request restriction of processing in certain circumstances

docs.title: 4.3 Request Process
docs.text: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us using the "Contact Information" provided below. We will respond to legitimate requests within 30 days.

docs.title: 5.1 Security Measures
docs.text:
• Encryption: All data transmission is encrypted using industry-standard protocols
  • Access Controls: Strict access controls limit who can access user data
  • Regular Audits: Regular security audits and vulnerability assessments
  • Secure Infrastructure: Hosted on secure, monitored infrastructure

docs.title: 5.2 Data Minimization
docs.text: We collect only the data necessary to provide the Application's features and maintain only what is needed for operational purposes.

docs.title: 6. Children's Privacy
docs.text: The Application is not intended for use by individuals under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If we become aware that we have collected personal information from a child under 13, we will take steps to delete such information.

docs.title: 7. International Data Transfers
docs.text: Your data may be transferred to and processed in countries other than your own. These countries may have different data protection laws. We ensure appropriate safeguards are in place for such transfers.

docs.title: 8. Contact Information
docs.text:
If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy, please contact the developer:

  • Email: james911129@gmail.com
  • Discord Support Server: https://discord.gg/BvP64mqKzR
  • GitHub Repository: https://github.com/starpig1129/ai-bot

docs.title: 9. Policy Changes
docs.text:
We reserve the right to modify this Privacy Policy at any time. Any significant changes will be:

  • Posted on this page with an updated effective date
  • Announced on the Application's official Discord Support Server
  • Provided via direct notification for material changes

We encourage you to review this Privacy Policy periodically to stay informed about how we protect your information.

This Privacy Policy is effective as of the date stated above and governs our collection, use, and disclosure of your personal information.

docs.title: Addons System
docs.text:
Directory: addons/

The Addons System is the core infrastructure module that provides essential services and functionality for the PigPig Discord Bot. It manages system configuration, security tokens, and the automatic update system.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The addons system is organized into the following components:

  • Core Configuration: Centralized configuration management for all bot subsystems
  • Security & Authentication: Token management and environment variable handling
  • Automatic Updates: Comprehensive update system with security, backup, and rollback capabilities
  • System Integration: Seamless integration between different bot components

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
graph TB
    A[addons/__init__.py] --> B[settings.py]
    A --> C[tokens.py]
    A --> D[update/]

    B --> E[BaseConfig]
    B --> F[LLMConfig]
    B --> G[UpdateConfig]
    B --> H[MusicConfig]
    B --> I[PromptConfig]
    B --> J[MemoryConfig]

    D --> K[checker.py]
    D --> L[downloader.py]
    D --> M[manager.py]
    D --> N[notifier.py]
    D --> O[restart.py]
    D --> P[security.py]

docs.title: Configuration Management
docs.text:
The configuration system provides centralized management for all bot settings:

  • BaseConfig: Basic bot configuration (prefix, activity, etc.)
  • LLMConfig: Language model priorities and AI service settings
  • UpdateConfig: Auto-update system configuration
  • MusicConfig: Music system and FFmpeg settings
  • PromptConfig: System prompt and agent configuration
  • MemoryConfig: Memory subsystem configuration

docs.title: Security & Tokens
docs.text:
• tokens.py: Environment variable management and API key handling
  • Secure authentication for Discord bot operations
  • Integration with third-party AI services (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google)

docs.title: Update System
docs.text:
• Version Checker: GitHub version comparison and release detection
  • Update Downloader: Secure file download with verification
  • Update Manager: Core orchestration of the update process
  • Security Module: Permission checking, backup management, and rollback
  • Notification System: Discord-based update notifications
  • Restart Manager: System restart after updates

docs.title: 1. Configuration Management
docs.text:
• YAML-based Configuration: All settings loaded from base_configs/ directory
  • Environment Variable Integration: Seamless .env file support
  • Error Handling: Robust error reporting with func.report_error()
  • Lazy Loading: Configurations loaded on demand for optimal performance

docs.title: 2. Security Features
docs.text:
• Permission Validation: Only bot owners can perform updates
  • Backup & Rollback: Automatic backup before updates with rollback capability
  • Protected Files: Config files and sensitive data protected during updates
  • Environment Validation: Comprehensive checking of required environment variables

docs.title: 3. Update System
docs.text:
• Automatic Version Checking: Periodic checks for GitHub releases
  • Secure Downloads: Verified file downloads with integrity checking
  • Progress Tracking: Real-time update progress notifications
  • Graceful Restart: Smooth bot restart after successful updates
  • Error Recovery: Automatic rollback on update failures

docs.title: 4. Cross-System Integration
docs.text:
• Module Exports: Clean separation via __all__ declarations
  • Lazy Imports: Avoid circular dependencies through delayed imports
  • Consistent Error Handling: Unified error reporting across all modules

docs.title: Configuration Access
docs.text:
from addons import base_config, llm_config, update_config

# Access base bot configuration
print(f"Bot prefix: {base_config.prefix}")

# Check update settings
if update_config.auto_update.get("enabled", False):
    print("Auto-update is enabled")

docs.title: Token Management
docs.text:
from addons import tokens

# Access environment variables
bot_token = tokens.token
owner_id = tokens.bot_owner_id

# Use in bot initialization
client.run(bot_token)

docs.title: Update System Integration
docs.text:
from addons.update import UpdateManager

# Initialize update manager
update_manager = UpdateManager(bot)

# Check for updates
update_info = await update_manager.check_for_updates()

# Execute update if available
if update_info.get("update_available"):
    result = await update_manager.execute_update()

docs.title: Configuration Structure
docs.text:
The addons system expects the following configuration structure:

base_configs/
├── base.yaml          # Basic bot settings
├── llm.yaml           # AI model configuration
├── update.yaml        # Update system settings
├── music.yaml         # Music system settings
├── prompt/            # Prompt templates
│   ├── default.yaml
│   └── ...
└── memory.yaml        # Memory subsystem settings

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
All modules in the addons system follow consistent error handling patterns:

  • Primary: Use func.report_error() for unified error reporting
  • Fallback: Local logging when the primary system is unavailable
  • Graceful Degradation: Continue operation with warnings when possible

docs.title: Security Considerations
docs.text:
1. Environment Variables: All sensitive data loaded from .env files
  2. Permission Control: Update operations restricted to bot owners
  3. Backup Protection: Critical files protected during updates
  4. Input Validation: All external data validated before use
  5. Secure Downloads: File integrity verified before installation

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
The addons system depends on:

  • Core: yaml, dotenv, asyncio, logging
  • Update System: aiohttp, zipfile, hashlib
  • Security: discord.py, shutil
  • Integration: function module for unified error reporting

docs.title: Maintenance
docs.text:
For maintenance and troubleshooting:

  1. Configuration Issues: Check base_configs/ directory and YAML syntax
  2. Update Failures: Review data/update_logs/ for detailed logs
  3. Permission Errors: Verify BOT_OWNER_ID environment variable
  4. Network Issues: Check GitHub API connectivity and rate limits

For detailed documentation on specific components, see the individual module documentation.

docs.title: Logging Manager (addons/logging_manager.py)
docs.text: This document describes the new structured logging system used by the project. The implementation lives in addons/logging_manager.py and is configured via addons/settings.py (see BaseConfig.logging).

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Outputs structured NDJSON to per-server, per-day files: logs/<server_id>/<YYYYMMDD>/bot_log_<LEVEL>.jsonl.
  • Console output is a single-line human readable format: [timestamp][level][source][channel_or_file][user_id] action=... message=...
  • Background writer batches writes for high throughput and low syscall overhead.
  • Public API: get_logger(server_id, source='server', channel=None) -> logger-like adapter

docs.title: Getting a Logger
docs.text:
Import and create a logger for a server (or "system"):

python from addons.logging_manager import get_logger

log = get_logger(server_id=123, source="server", channel="general")

The returned object supports: - .info(message, exception=None, event_fields) - .warning(...) - .error(...) - .debug(...) - .bind(context) -> returns a new adapter with merged bound context

Example: python log.info("User sent message", user_id=456, action="send", message="hello", trace_id="abc123")

docs.title: Structured NDJSON Schema
docs.text:
Each line is a single JSON object with fields:

  • timestamp: ISO 8601 UTC (e.g. 2025-11-17T08:00:00Z)
  • level: INFO/WARNING/ERROR/DEBUG
  • source: server/system/external
  • server_id: string or integer
  • channel_or_file: string
  • user_id: string or integer
  • action: short verb
  • message: text
  • trace_id: optional request/correlation id
  • extra: dictionary with any vendor-specific fields

Example NDJSON line: {"timestamp":"2025-11-17T08:00:00Z","level":"INFO","source":"server","server_id":"123","channel_or_file":"general","user_id":"456","action":"send","message":"hello","trace_id":"abc123","extra":{}}

docs.title: Console Renderer
docs.text:
Console single-line format (used when console enabled in config):

[timestamp][level][source][channel_or_file][user_id] action=... message=...

Colorization is applied using the color_map in configuration and is handled by loguru tags.

docs.title: Configuration (addons/settings.py)
docs.text:
The logging configuration is exposed via addons.settings.base_config.logging (loaded into addons.settings.BaseConfig.logging). Key options:

  • console:
  • enabled: bool — enable console rendering

  • color: bool — use color tags for console output

  • color_map:

  • level: map from level name (e.g., "ERROR") to color name (e.g., "red")

  • source: map from source (e.g., "system") to color name

  • async:

  • batch_size: int — number of records to batch before write

  • flush_interval: float — seconds to wait before flushing if batch not full

  • rotation:

  • policy: "daily" (informational; rotation implemented externally)
  • compress: bool

  • retention_days: int

  • per_level_retention:

  • mapping for retention days by level, e.g. {"INFO": 300, "ERROR": 900}

  • fsync_on_flush: bool — best-effort fsync on each flush (off by default)

  • log_base_path: string — base directory for logs (default: "logs")

Example configuration fragment (placed in base YAML under logging section): python LOGGING = { "console": {"enabled": True, "color": True}, "color_map": { "level": {"ERROR": "red", "WARNING": "yellow", "INFO": "green", "DEBUG": "cyan"}, "source": {"system": "magenta", "external": "blue"} }, "async": {"batch_size": 500, "flush_interval": 2.0}, "rotation": {"policy": "daily", "compress": True, "retention_days": 300}, "per_level_retention": {"INFO": 300, "WARNING": 300, "ERROR": 900}, "fsync_on_flush": False, "log_base_path": "logs", }

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
Unit and integration tests for the logging manager were added under tests/test_logging_manager.py. Tests include: - NDJSON serialization correctness - Console line format correctness - Background writer writes per-level files - A light-load integration test that writes multiple events and verifies file output

See tests/test_logging_manager.py for implementation details.

docs.title: Operational Notes
docs.text:
• Background writer runs in a dedicated daemon thread and uses a bounded queue to avoid unbounded memory growth. When the queue is full events are dropped and reported using func.report_error (project-wide error reporting helper).
  • For retention and rotation, perform daily compression and cleanup with a scheduled job or small maintenance script; these operations are intentionally kept out of the write path for performance.
  • Do not log sensitive PII or tokens. Add redaction hooks prior to emitting structured events if necessary.

docs.title: Migration
docs.text: The compatibility shim logs.py was used during migration but should be removed once all code uses get_logger. The new API is stable and recommended for all modules.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• Implementation: addons/logging_manager.py
  • Configuration object: addons/settings.py
  • Design plan: plan.md

docs.title: Settings Module
docs.text:
File: addons/settings.py

The Settings module is the comprehensive configuration management system for the PigPig Discord Bot. It provides centralized access to all bot settings through YAML configuration files and environment variables, supporting multiple configuration domains including bot settings, AI models, updates, music, prompts, and memory systems.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The settings module implements a robust configuration system that: - Loads settings from YAML files in the CONFIG_ROOT directory - Provides type-safe configuration objects for each domain - Implements graceful error handling with fallback mechanisms - Supports both required and optional configuration parameters - Integrates with environment variables for sensitive data

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
graph TB
    A[settings.py] --> B[BaseConfig]
    A --> C[LLMConfig]
    A --> D[UpdateConfig]
    A --> E[MusicConfig]
    A --> F[PromptConfig]
    A --> G[MemoryConfig]

    B --> H[YAML Loading]
    C --> H
    D --> H
    E --> H
    F --> H
    G --> H

    H --> I[Environment Variables]
    H --> J[Error Handling]

docs.title: BaseConfig
docs.text:
Configuration File: CONFIG_ROOT/base.yaml

Manages basic bot configuration including commands, activity, and system settings.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• prefix (str): Command prefix for bot commands (default: "/")
  • activity (list): List of bot activity configurations
  • ipc_server (dict): IPC server configuration settings
  • version (str): Current bot version string
  • logging (dict): Logging configuration settings

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
from addons import base_config

# Access configuration properties
bot_prefix = base_config.prefix
activities = base_config.activity
version = base_config.version
ipc_config = base_config.ipc_server

docs.title: LLMConfig
docs.text:
Configuration File: CONFIG_ROOT/llm.yaml

Manages language model configuration and AI service settings.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• model_priorities (list): Priority order for language models
  • google_search_agent (str): Default Google search agent model name (default: "gemini-2.0-flash")

docs.title: Data Structure
docs.text:
{
    "model_priorities": ["gemini-2.0-flash", "gpt-4", "claude-3"],
    "google_search_agent": "gemini-2.0-flash"
}

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
from addons import llm_config

# Access LLM configuration
priorities = llm_config.model_priorities
default_agent = llm_config.google_search_agent

# Use in AI service initialization
model = llm_config.model_priorities[0]

docs.title: UpdateConfig
docs.text:
Configuration File: CONFIG_ROOT/update.yaml

Manages the automatic update system configuration.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• auto_update (dict): Auto-update system settings
  • security (dict): Security and backup configuration
  • notification (dict): Update notification settings
  • restart (dict): System restart configuration
  • github (dict): GitHub repository configuration

docs.title: Configuration Structure
docs.text:
auto_update:
  enabled: true
  check_interval: 3600
  require_owner_confirmation: true

security:
  backup_enabled: true
  protected_files:
    - ".env"
    - "data/"
    - "config/"
  max_backups: 5

github:
  api_url: "https://api.github.com/repos/starpig1129/ai-discord-bot-PigPig/releases/latest"

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
from addons import update_config

# Check if auto-update is enabled
if update_config.auto_update.get("enabled"):
    interval = update_config.auto_update.get("check_interval", 3600)
    print(f"Auto-update enabled, check interval: {interval}s")

# Access security settings
backup_enabled = update_config.security.get("backup_enabled", False)
protected_files = update_config.security.get("protected_files", [])

docs.title: MusicConfig
docs.text:
Configuration File: CONFIG_ROOT/music.yaml

Manages music system configuration including temporary files and FFmpeg settings.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• music_temp_base (str): Base directory for temporary music files (default: "temp/music")
  • ffmpeg (dict): FFmpeg configuration settings
  • youtube_cookies_path (str): Path to YouTube cookies file (default: "./data/youtube_cookies.txt")

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
from addons.settings import MusicConfig

music_config = MusicConfig()

# Access music settings
temp_dir = music_config.music_temp_base
cookies_path = music_config.youtube_cookies_path
ffmpeg_config = music_config.ffmpeg

docs.title: PromptConfig
docs.text:
Configuration File: CONFIG_ROOT/prompt/

Manages system prompts and agent configurations for different AI behaviors.

docs.title: Configuration Structure
docs.text:
prompt/
├── default.yaml
├── assistant.yaml
├── creative.yaml
└── ...

docs.title: get_system_prompt(agent_name: str, bot_id: int | None = None, message=None) -> str
docs.text:
Retrieves and formats system prompts for specific agents with variable substitution.

Parameters: - agent_name: Name of the agent configuration file - bot_id: Optional bot ID for variable substitution - message: Optional Discord message object

Returns: Formatted system prompt string

Supported Variables: - {bot_id}: Discord bot ID - {bot_owner_id}: Bot owner's Discord ID - {bot_name}: Bot name from configuration - {creator}: Bot creator name - {environment}: Operating environment description

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
from addons.settings import PromptConfig

prompt_config = PromptConfig()

# Get system prompt for specific agent
system_prompt = prompt_config.get_system_prompt("default", bot_id=123456789)

# Use in AI client initialization
client = anthropic.Anthropic()
response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-haiku-20240307",
    max_tokens=1024,
    system=system_prompt,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)

docs.title: MemoryConfig
docs.text:
Configuration File: CONFIG_ROOT/memory.yaml

Manages the memory subsystem configuration for persistent conversation storage.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• enabled (bool): Whether memory system is enabled (default: true)
  • procedural_cache_ttl (float): In-process procedural cache TTL in seconds (default: 300.0)
  • procedural_data_path (str): Path to procedural memory database
  • episodic_data_path (str): Path to episodic memory database
  • vector_store_type (str): Vector store provider (default: "qdrant")
  • qdrant_url (str): Qdrant vector store URL
  • qdrant_api_key (str | None): Qdrant API key
  • qdrant_collection_name (str): Vector collection name
  • embedding_provider (str): Embedding provider (default: "google")
  • embedding_model_name (str): Default embedding model
  • embedding_dim (int): Embedding dimensions (default: 768)
  • vector_search_k (int): Number of vector search results (default: 5)
  • keyword_search_k (int): Number of keyword search results (default: 5)
  • ollama_url (str | None): Ollama service URL
  • provider_options (dict): Provider-specific options
  • message_threshold (int): Messages before triggering memory processing

docs.title: Configuration Example
docs.text:
enabled: true
procedural_cache_ttl: 300.0
procedural_data_path: "data/memory/procedural.db"
episodic_data_path: "data/memory/episodic.db"
vector_store_type: "qdrant"
qdrant_url: "http://localhost:6333"
qdrant_api_key: null
qdrant_collection_name: "ephemeral_memory"
embedding_provider: "google"
embedding_model_name: "gemini-embedding-001"
embedding_dim: 768
vector_search_k: 5
keyword_search_k: 5
ollama_url: "http://localhost:11434"
provider_options: {}
message_threshold: 100

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
from addons.settings import MemoryConfig

memory_config = MemoryConfig()

# Check if memory system is enabled
if memory_config.enabled:
    print(f"Using {memory_config.vector_store_type} vector store")
    print(f"Embedding model: {memory_config.embedding_model_name}")

    # Access configuration for memory system initialization
    config = {
        "vector_store_url": memory_config.qdrant_url,
        "collection_name": memory_config.qdrant_collection_name,
        "embedding_provider": memory_config.embedding_provider
    }

docs.title: Configuration File Structure
docs.text:
The settings module expects the following directory structure:

CONFIG_ROOT/
├── base.yaml          # Basic bot configuration
├── llm.yaml           # Language model settings
├── update.yaml        # Update system configuration
├── music.yaml         # Music system settings
├── prompt/            # System prompts
│   ├── default.yaml
│   ├── assistant.yaml
│   └── ...
└── memory.yaml        # Memory subsystem settings

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: The module implements comprehensive error handling:

docs.title: Primary Error Reporting
docs.text:
try:
    from function import func
    asyncio.create_task(func.report_error(e, "addons/settings.py/method_name"))
except Exception:
    # Fallback to local logging
    print(f"Configuration error: {e}")

docs.title: Graceful Degradation
docs.text:
• Missing Files: Use empty dictionaries with default values
  • Invalid YAML: Log warnings and use empty configurations
  • Environment Issues: Fall back to default configuration paths

docs.title: Error Scenarios
docs.text:
1. Missing Configuration Files: Module continues with empty configurations
  2. Invalid YAML Syntax: Uses fallback empty dictionaries
  3. Environment Variable Issues: Uses default configuration root
  4. Permission Errors: Logs warnings and attempts alternative approaches

docs.title: 1. Environment Setup
docs.text:
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()  # Load .env file

docs.title: 2. Root Configuration Detection
docs.text:
def _get_config_root() -> str:
    root = os.getenv("CONFIG_ROOT")
    if root is None:
        # Report error and fall back to default
        root = "./base_configs"
    return root.rstrip("/\\")

docs.title: 3. Individual Configuration Loading
docs.text: Each configuration class: 1. Loads YAML file from determined root 2. Applies default values for missing keys 3. Handles errors gracefully with logging 4. Provides type-safe access to configuration data

docs.title: Standard Import Pattern
docs.text:
from addons import base_config, llm_config, update_config

# Access configuration
prefix = base_config.prefix
models = llm_config.model_priorities
update_enabled = update_config.auto_update.get("enabled", False)

docs.title: Custom Configuration Access
docs.text:
from addons.settings import MusicConfig, PromptConfig

music_config = MusicConfig()
prompt_config = PromptConfig()

# Access specific configurations
temp_dir = music_config.music_temp_base
system_prompt = prompt_config.get_system_prompt("default")

docs.title: Configuration Validation
docs.text:
def validate_configurations():
    """Validate that all configurations are properly loaded"""

    # Check basic configuration
    if not hasattr(base_config, 'prefix'):
        raise ValueError("Base configuration not properly loaded")

    # Check update configuration
    if not hasattr(update_config, 'auto_update'):
        raise ValueError("Update configuration not properly loaded")

    return True

docs.title: With TOKENS Module
docs.text:
from addons.tokens import tokens
from addons.settings import MemoryConfig

memory_config = MemoryConfig()

# Use bot owner ID from tokens
owner_id = getattr(tokens, "bot_owner_id", 0)

docs.title: With Update System
docs.text:
from addons.update import UpdateManager
from addons.settings import UpdateConfig

update_config = UpdateConfig()
update_manager = UpdateManager(bot, update_config)

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use Pre-initialized Instances: Prefer base_config over creating new instances
  2. Handle Missing Keys: Always provide defaults when accessing configuration
  3. Validate Configuration: Check required configurations on startup
  4. Environment Separation: Use different configuration files for dev/prod
  5. Error Handling: Always handle configuration loading failures gracefully

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
"CONFIG_ROOT environment variable not set" - Set CONFIG_ROOT environment variable to your configuration directory - Or ensure base_configs/ directory exists in the project root

"YAML loading failed" - Check YAML syntax in configuration files - Ensure files are valid UTF-8 encoded text - Verify file permissions allow reading

"Configuration attributes missing" - Check that all expected configuration files exist - Verify YAML structure matches expected format - Check for typos in configuration property names

docs.title: Debug Configuration Loading
docs.text:
import os
from addons.settings import CONFIG_ROOT

print(f"Configuration root: {CONFIG_ROOT}")
print(f"Environment CONFIG_ROOT: {os.getenv('CONFIG_ROOT')}")

# Check if configuration files exist
config_files = ["base.yaml", "llm.yaml", "update.yaml"]
for file in config_files:
    path = f"{CONFIG_ROOT}/{file}"
    print(f"{file}: {'EXISTS' if os.path.exists(path) else 'MISSING'}")

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• Tokens Module: Environment variable management
  • Update System: Automatic update system configuration
  • Bot Integration Guide: Complete setup instructions

The settings module provides a robust, scalable foundation for configuration management while maintaining simplicity and reliability.

docs.title: TOKENS Module
docs.text:
File: addons/tokens.py

The TOKENS module is responsible for managing all environment variables, API keys, and sensitive authentication tokens required for the Discord bot and its integrations. It provides secure loading and validation of sensitive configuration data.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The TOKENS class centralizes all authentication and API key management, ensuring that sensitive data is: - Loaded securely from environment variables - Validated before bot startup - Protected from accidental exposure - Used consistently across the application

docs.title: Initialization
docs.text:
class TOKENS:
    def __init__(self) -> None:

The TOKENS class automatically loads and validates all required environment variables upon instantiation.

docs.title: Core Bot Authentication
docs.text:
• token (str): Discord bot token for bot authentication
  • client_id (str): Discord application client ID
  • client_secret_id (str): Discord application client secret ID
  • sercet_key (str): Secret key for bot operations

docs.title: User Management
docs.text:
• bot_owner_id (int): Discord user ID of the bot owner (0 if not set)
  • bug_report_channel_id (int | None): Discord channel ID for bug reports

docs.title: AI Service API Keys
docs.text:
• anthropic_api_key (str | None): Anthropic Claude API key
  • openai_api_key (str | None): OpenAI API key
  • google_api_key (str | None): Google API key (Gemini services)
  • tenor_api_key (str | None): Tenor GIF API key
  • vector_store_api_key (str | None): Vector store API key (for memory system)

docs.title: Environment Variable Loading
docs.text:
The module automatically loads environment variables using python-dotenv:

from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()  # Loads .env file automatically

docs.title: Required Environment Variables
docs.text:
The following environment variables are required for bot operation:

TOKEN=your_discord_bot_token
CLIENT_ID=your_discord_client_id
CLIENT_SECRET_ID=your_discord_client_secret
SERCET_KEY=your_secret_key
BOT_OWNER_ID=your_user_id
BUG_REPORT_CHANNEL_ID=channel_id_for_bugs

docs.title: Optional API Keys
docs.text:
These environment variables are optional but enable additional functionality:

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_anthropic_key
OPENAI_API_KEY=your_openai_key
GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_google_key
TENOR_API_KEY=your_tenor_key
VECTOR_STORE_API_KEY=your_vector_store_key

docs.title: validate_environment_variables() Method
docs.text:
The module performs comprehensive validation of all environment variables:

  1. Required Variables Check: Verifies all essential variables are present
  2. Data Type Validation: Ensures numeric IDs are valid integers
  3. API Key Warning: Logs warnings for missing optional API keys
  4. Critical Error Handling: Exits with SystemExit if required variables are missing

docs.title: Validation Rules
docs.text:
# Required variables must not be empty
required_vars = {
    "TOKEN": self.token,
    "CLIENT_ID": self.client_id,
    "CLIENT_SECRET_ID": self.client_secret_id,
    "SERCET_KEY": self.sercet_key,
    "BUG_REPORT_CHANNEL_ID": os.getenv("BUG_REPORT_CHANNEL_ID"),
    "BOT_OWNER_ID": os.getenv("BOT_OWNER_ID"),
}

# Numeric validation for IDs
if var_name == "BUG_REPORT_CHANNEL_ID":
    try:
        int(var_value)
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        invalid_vars.append(f"{var_name} (must be valid integer)")

docs.title: Error Handling Strategy
docs.text:
1. Primary: Use func.report_error() for unified error reporting
  2. Fallback: Print to stdout if error reporting system is unavailable
  3. Critical: Exit with SystemExit(1) if required variables are missing

docs.title: Basic Token Access
docs.text:
from addons.tokens import TOKENS, tokens

# Create instance (loads all environment variables)
tokens_instance = TOKENS()

# Access individual tokens
discord_token = tokens_instance.token
owner_id = tokens_instance.bot_owner_id
bug_channel = tokens_instance.bug_report_channel_id

# Check optional API keys
if tokens_instance.anthropic_api_key:
    print("Anthropic integration enabled")

docs.title: Using Pre-initialized Instance
docs.text:
from addons import tokens

# Use the pre-initialized instance
if tokens.anthropic_api_key:
    # Initialize Anthropic client
    client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=tokens.anthropic_api_key)

docs.title: Environment Variable Setup
docs.text:
Create a .env file in your project root:

# Discord Bot Configuration
TOKEN=your_discord_bot_token_here
CLIENT_ID=your_client_id_here
CLIENT_SECRET_ID=your_client_secret_here
SERCET_KEY=your_secret_key_here

# Bot Management
BOT_OWNER_ID=123456789012345678
BUG_REPORT_CHANNEL_ID=123456789012345678

# Optional API Keys
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_anthropic_key
OPENAI_API_KEY=your_openai_key
GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_google_key
TENOR_API_KEY=your_tenor_key
VECTOR_STORE_API_KEY=your_vector_store_key

docs.title: Integration with Discord Bot
docs.text:
import discord
from addons.tokens import tokens

intents = discord.Intents.default()
intents.message_content = True

client = discord.Client(intents=intents)

@client.event
async def on_ready():
    print(f'Bot logged in as {client.user}')

# Use token from TOKENS instance
client.run(tokens.token)

docs.title: Error Reporting Integration
docs.text:
The TOKENS module integrates with the global error reporting system:

try:
    from function import func
    asyncio.create_task(func.report_error(error, "addons/tokens.py/method_name"))
except Exception:
    # Fallback to local logging
    print(f"Error in TOKENS module: {e}")

docs.title: Configuration Loading
docs.text:
Used by other modules for authentication:

# In settings.py
from addons.tokens import tokens
bot_owner_id = getattr(tokens, "bot_owner_id", 0)

docs.title: Missing Required Variables
docs.text: If required environment variables are missing: 1. Error logged via func.report_error() 2. User-friendly error message displayed 3. Program exits with SystemExit(1)

docs.title: Invalid Data Types
docs.text: For invalid numeric IDs (non-integer values): 1. Specific error message indicates the problematic variable 2. Program exits to prevent runtime errors

docs.title: Optional API Keys Missing
docs.text: For optional API keys: 1. Warning logged without stopping execution 2. Features that require those keys will be disabled 3. Bot operates with available functionality

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Environment Files: Always use .env files, never hardcode credentials
  2. File Permissions: Restrict access to .env files (chmod 600)
  3. Version Control: Add .env to .gitignore
  4. Key Rotation: Regularly rotate API keys and tokens
  5. Monitoring: Monitor API usage and key exposure

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
"Environment variable validation failed" - Check that all required variables are set in .env - Verify variable names match exactly (case-sensitive) - Ensure no trailing spaces or quotes

"BOT_OWNER_ID must be valid integer" - Check that BOT_OWNER_ID contains only digits - Remove any formatting (commas, spaces, etc.)

"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set" - This is a warning, not an error - Bot will work but Claude integration disabled - Set the key to enable Anthropic features

docs.title: Debugging Environment Variables
docs.text:
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

# Print all relevant variables (remove sensitive data in production)
print("Bot Owner ID:", os.getenv("BOT_OWNER_ID"))
print("Client ID:", os.getenv("CLIENT_ID"))
print("Has Token:", bool(os.getenv("TOKEN")))

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• Settings Module: Configuration management
  • Update System: Automatic update system
  • Error Handling Guide: Centralized error reporting

Note: Always handle tokens and API keys with extreme care. Never commit them to version control or expose them in logs.

docs.title: Update System
docs.text: The update system, located in the addons/update/ directory, is a comprehensive module responsible for managing automatic updates for the bot. It handles version checking, downloading, security, and notifications.

docs.title: Modules
docs.text:
This system is composed of the following modules:

  • Checker: Checks for new versions on GitHub.
  • Downloader: Securely downloads update files.
  • Manager: The core orchestrator of the update process.
  • Notifier: Sends update-related notifications via Discord.
  • Restart: Manages the graceful restart of the bot after an update.
  • Security: Handles permissions, backups, and configuration protection.

docs.title: Checker Module
docs.text:
File: addons/update/checker.py

This module is responsible for checking for new versions of the bot on GitHub. It compares the local version with the latest release available in the repository.

docs.title: VersionChecker Class
docs.text: The VersionChecker class handles the logic for checking versions.

docs.title: __init__(self, github_config: Dict[str, str])
docs.text:
Initializes the VersionChecker.

  • Parameters:
    • github_config (Dict[str, str]): A dictionary containing GitHub configuration, including the api_url.

docs.title: async check_for_updates(self) -> Dict[str, any]
docs.text:
Checks for available updates by querying the GitHub API.

  • Returns: A dictionary containing version information and the update status. The dictionary includes keys such as current_version, latest_version, update_available, release_notes, and download_url.

docs.title: get_current_version(self) -> str
docs.text:
Retrieves the current version of the bot.

  • Returns: The current version string.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
```python import asyncio from addons.update.checker import VersionChecker

async def main(): # Example GitHub configuration github_config = { "api_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/starpig1129/ai-discord-bot-PigPig/releases/latest" }

checker = VersionChecker(github_config)
update_info = await checker.check_for_updates()

if update_info.get("update_available"):
    print(f"New version available: {update_info['latest_version']}")
    print(f"Release notes: {update_info['release_notes']}")
else:
    print("You are using the latest version.")

if name == "main": asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Downloader Module
docs.text:
File: addons/update/downloader.py

This module is responsible for securely downloading update files from a given URL. It includes features for progress tracking, file verification, and cleanup of old downloads.

docs.title: UpdateDownloader Class
docs.text: The UpdateDownloader class manages the download process.

docs.title: __init__(self, download_dir: str = "temp/downloads")
docs.text:
Initializes the UpdateDownloader.

  • Parameters:
    • download_dir (str): The directory where update files will be stored. Defaults to "temp/downloads".

docs.title: async download_update(self, download_url: str, progress_callback: Optional[Callable[[int], Awaitable[None]]] = None, chunk_size: int = 8192) -> str
docs.text:
Downloads an update file from the specified URL.

  • Parameters:
    • download_url (str): The URL of the update file.
    • progress_callback (Optional[Callable[[int], Awaitable[None]]]): An optional async callback function to report download progress (0-100).
    • chunk_size (int): The size of download chunks in bytes.
  • Returns: The file path of the downloaded update.
  • Raises: Exception if the download fails or the file verification is unsuccessful.

docs.title: calculate_file_hash(self, filepath: str, algorithm: str = 'sha256') -> str
docs.text:
Calculates the hash of a file.

  • Parameters:
    • filepath (str): The path to the file.
    • algorithm (str): The hashing algorithm to use (e.g., 'sha256').
  • Returns: The hexadecimal hash string of the file.

docs.title: cleanup_downloads(self, keep_latest: int = 3) -> None
docs.text:
Cleans up old update files in the download directory.

  • Parameters:
    • keep_latest (int): The number of latest downloads to keep.

docs.title: get_download_dir(self) -> str
docs.text:
Gets the path to the download directory.

  • Returns: The download directory path.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
```python import asyncio from addons.update.downloader import UpdateDownloader

async def progress_reporter(progress): print(f"Download progress: {progress}%")

async def main(): downloader = UpdateDownloader() download_url = "https://github.com/starpig1129/ai-discord-bot-PigPig/archive/refs/tags/v2.0.0.zip" # Example URL

try:
    download_path = await downloader.download_update(download_url, progress_callback=progress_reporter)
    print(f"Update downloaded to: {download_path}")

    # Clean up old downloads, keeping the 3 most recent
    downloader.cleanup_downloads(keep_latest=3)
except Exception as e:
    print(f"An error occurred: {e}")

if name == "main": asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Manager Module
docs.text:
File: addons/update/manager.py

This is the core module of the update system. The UpdateManager class orchestrates the entire update process, integrating all other components of the system, such as the version checker, downloader, and notifier.

docs.title: UpdateManager Class
docs.text: This class provides a unified interface for managing the full update lifecycle.

docs.title: __init__(self, bot)
docs.text:
Initializes the UpdateManager and all its components.

  • Parameters:
    • bot: The instance of the Discord bot.

docs.title: async check_for_updates(self) -> Dict[str, Any]
docs.text:
Checks for available updates.

  • Returns: A dictionary containing version information. See VersionChecker.check_for_updates() for details.

docs.title: async execute_update(self, interaction=None, force: bool = False) -> Dict[str, Any]
docs.text:
Executes the full update process. This is a comprehensive workflow that includes: 1. Checking for updates. 2. Creating a backup (if enabled). 3. Downloading the new version. 4. Installing the update. 5. Cleaning up old backups and downloaded files. 6. Notifying the owner of the result. 7. Initiating a graceful restart.

  • Parameters:
    • interaction (Optional): The Discord interaction object that triggered the update.
    • force (bool): If True, the update will be attempted even if no new version is detected.
  • Returns: A dictionary containing the results of the update, including a success flag and other relevant details.

docs.title: get_status(self) -> Dict[str, Any]
docs.text:
Gets the current status of the update system.

  • Returns: A dictionary with status information, such as status, progress, operation, and current_version.

docs.title: async post_restart_initialization(self)
docs.text: Performs necessary checks and initializations after the bot has restarted. This is typically called once upon bot startup.

• current_status (str): The current status (e.g., "idle", "checking", "downloading", "error").
  • progress (int): The progress percentage of the current operation.
  • current_operation (str): A description of the current operation.
  • error_message (Optional[str]): An error message if the process has failed.

docs.text:

  • 稍後提醒 (Remind Later): Dismisses the current notification, which will reappear at the next update check.

docs.title: Restart Module
docs.text:
File: addons/update/restart.py

This module provides a simple and reliable mechanism for restarting the bot, which is crucial after an update. It is designed to be robust, using system-level commands to ensure the restart process completes successfully.

docs.title: SimpleRestartManager Class
docs.text: This class (also aliased as GracefulRestartManager) manages the entire restart process.

docs.title: __init__(self, bot, restart_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None)
docs.text:
Initializes the restart manager.

  • Parameters:
    • bot: The instance of the Discord bot.
    • restart_config (Optional[Dict[str, Any]]): A dictionary containing restart configuration, such as the path for the restart flag file and delay settings.

docs.title: async execute_restart(self, reason: str = "update_restart") -> None
docs.text:
Executes the restart process. This involves saving a restart flag, shutting down the bot gracefully, and then executing a system-specific command to start the bot again.

  • Parameters:
    • reason (str): The reason for the restart, which is logged.

docs.title: async post_restart_check(self) -> bool
docs.text:
Performs a check after the bot has restarted. It looks for the restart flag file to confirm that the restart was intentional and then runs a simple health check.

  • Returns: True if the check passes or if it was a normal startup, False if the health check fails.

docs.title: is_restart_pending(self) -> bool
docs.text:
Checks if a restart is pending by looking for the existence of the restart flag file.

  • Returns: True if a restart is pending, False otherwise.

docs.title: cancel_restart(self) -> bool
docs.text:
Cancels a pending restart by deleting the restart flag file.

  • Returns: True if the restart was successfully canceled, False otherwise.

docs.text: ```python

docs.title: This class is typically used internally by the UpdateManager.
The following is a conceptual example.
docs.text: import asyncio from addons.update.restart import SimpleRestartManager

docs.title: bot = discord.Client()
docs.text:

print("Initiating restart...")
await restart_manager.execute_restart(reason="manual_restart")
print("This line will likely not be reached as the process exits.")

docs.title: Discord Bot Cogs Documentation
docs.text: This directory contains the documentation for all Discord bot cogs (extensions). Each cog is a self-contained module that provides specific functionality to the Discord bot.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The bot consists of 17 main cog modules plus several subdirectories containing specialized functionality. Each cog is designed to be modular and can be loaded independently.

docs.title: Core System Modules
docs.text:
• botinfo.md - Bot information display and system statistics
  • help.md - Command help system with multi-language support
  • language_manager.md - Multi-language translation system
  • channel_manager.md - Server and channel permission management

docs.title: Media & Entertainment
docs.text:
• music.md - YouTube music player with queue management
  • gen_img.md - AI-powered image generation using Gemini and local models
  • gif_tools.md - GIF search and management using Tenor API
  • summarizer.md - AI-powered conversation summarization

docs.title: Utility & Productivity
docs.text:
• math.md - Mathematical calculation engine using SymPy
  • remind.md - Time-based reminder system with natural language parsing
  • schedule.md - Personal schedule management with YAML file support
  • internet_search.md - Web search using Google Search API and web scraping

docs.title: Memory & Learning
docs.text:
• userdata.md - Personal user data management and preferences
  • episodic_memory.md - Episodic memory system for conversation context

docs.title: Content Management
docs.text:
• story_manager.md - Interactive story creation and management system
  • system_prompt_manager.md - Dynamic system prompt management per channel/guild
  • update_manager.md - Automatic bot update system

docs.title: eat/ - Food Recommendation System
docs.text:
• Purpose: Restaurant and food recommendation with AI-powered suggestions
  • Features: Location-based search, personal preferences learning, review analysis
  • Key Components: Google Maps integration, ML-based recommendation engine

docs.title: memory/ - Memory Management System
docs.text:
• Purpose: Advanced memory management for episodic and procedural memories
  • Features: Vector storage, message tracking, event summarization
  • Architecture: Modular storage interfaces, embedding providers, vector stores

docs.title: music_lib/ - Music System Library
docs.text:
• Purpose: Core music playback and queue management system
  • Components:
  • audio_manager.py - Audio processing and playback
  • queue_manager.py - Song queue and playlist management
  • state_manager.py - Player state persistence
  • ui_manager.py - User interface management
  • youtube.py - YouTube integration and audio downloading
  • ui/ - User interface components (buttons, progress bars, song selection)

docs.title: story/ - Story System Core
docs.text:
• Purpose: Interactive storytelling system with AI-powered narrative generation
  • Features: Multi-character stories, world building, relationship tracking
  • Architecture:
  • database.py - SQLite database for stories, characters, and relationships
  • manager.py - Core story processing and AI agent coordination
  • models.py - Data models and Pydantic schemas
  • prompt_engine.py - Dynamic prompt generation for AI agents
  • state_manager.py - Story state management
  • ui/ - User interface components (modals, views, UI manager)

docs.title: system_prompt/ - System Prompt Management
docs.text:
• Purpose: Dynamic system prompt management for AI conversations
  • Features: Per-channel/guild prompts, permission-based editing, template system
  • Components: Manager, commands, permissions, caching, validation

docs.title: Common Features
docs.text:
All cogs follow these design patterns:

  1. Multi-language Support: All user-facing text uses the LanguageManager for localization
  2. Error Handling: Consistent error reporting through func.report_error()
  3. Configuration: Settings loaded from addons/settings.py
  4. Async/Await: Full asynchronous operation for optimal performance
  5. Permission Checks: Proper Discord permission validation

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Core: discord.py, asyncio
  • AI/LLM: langchain, Google Generative AI SDK
  • Database: sqlite3, sqlalchemy
  • Media: PIL, yt-dlp, ffmpeg
  • Utilities: aiohttp, python-dateutil, sympy

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Each cog can be loaded independently using Discord's cog loading system:

# Load a specific cog
await bot.load_extension('cogs.music')

# Unload a cog  
await bot.unload_extension('cogs.music')

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Cog-specific configuration is managed through: - addons/settings.py - Main configuration file - base_configs/ - Base configuration templates - data/ - Runtime data storage - Database files - Persistent storage for user data

docs.title: Development Guidelines
docs.text:
When working with cogs:

  1. Follow PEP 8 style guidelines
  2. Use async/await for all I/O operations
  3. Implement proper error handling with func.report_error()
  4. Support multi-language through LanguageManager
  5. Validate permissions before executing privileged operations
  6. Use type hints for better code documentation
  7. Write comprehensive docstrings in English

docs.title: Command Structure
docs.text:
Most cogs provide both slash commands (/command) and support for traditional message-based commands where appropriate. Slash commands are preferred for better user experience and permission handling.

This documentation is automatically generated from the source code. For detailed implementation specifics, refer to individual module documentation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The BotInfo cog provides comprehensive bot information display and system monitoring capabilities. It offers both basic statistics and performance metrics through Discord embeds.

docs.title: Core Functionality
docs.text:
• Bot Statistics Display: Server count, user count, channel statistics
  • Performance Monitoring: Network latency, memory usage, uptime tracking
  • System Information: Bot status, loaded cogs, command counts
  • Multi-language Support: All text localized through LanguageManager

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• BotInfo class - Main cog implementation
  • botinfo slash command - Primary user interface
  • Uptime calculation and formatting
  • Memory usage monitoring
  • System resource tracking

docs.title: /botinfo
docs.text:
Displays comprehensive bot information including: - Basic Statistics: Server count, user count, text/voice channels - Performance Metrics: Network latency, memory usage, uptime - System Status: Bot status, loaded cogs, command counts

Parameters: None

Response: Rich embed with multiple sections of bot information

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The ChannelManager cog provides comprehensive server and channel permission management capabilities. It enables administrators to configure bot response modes, manage whitelists/blacklists, set channel-specific modes, and control automatic responses on a per-channel basis.

docs.title: Core Functionality
docs.text:
• Server-wide Response Modes: Unrestricted, whitelist, or blacklist modes
  • Channel-specific Modes: Special modes like story mode for individual channels
  • Whitelist/Blacklist Management: Fine-grained channel access control
  • Auto-response Configuration: Enable/disable automatic bot responses per channel
  • Multi-language Support: Fully localized administrative interface

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• ChannelManager class - Main cog implementation
  • JSON-based configuration storage
  • Permission validation system
  • Channel mode resolution logic

docs.title: /set_server_mode
docs.text:
Sets the server-wide response mode for all channels.

Parameters: - mode: Server response mode selection - unrestricted: Bot responds in all channels - whitelist: Bot responds only in whitelisted channels - blacklist: Bot responds in all channels except blacklisted ones

Permissions: Administrator only

docs.title: /set_channel_mode
docs.text:
Sets a special mode for a specific channel.

Parameters: - channel: Target Discord text channel - mode: Channel mode selection - default: Use server-wide settings - story: Enable story mode for interactive storytelling

Permissions: Administrator only

docs.title: /add_channel
docs.text:
Adds a channel to the whitelist or blacklist.

Parameters: - channel: Target Discord text channel - list_type: List type selection - whitelist: Add to allowed channels list - blacklist: Add to blocked channels list

Permissions: Administrator only

docs.title: /remove_channel
docs.text:
Removes a channel from the whitelist or blacklist.

Parameters: - channel: Target Discord text channel - list_type: List type selection - whitelist: Remove from allowed channels list - blacklist: Remove from blocked channels list

Permissions: Administrator only

docs.title: /auto_response
docs.text:
Enables or disables automatic bot responses for a specific channel.

Parameters: - channel: Target Discord text channel - enabled: Boolean to enable/disable auto-response

Permissions: Administrator only

docs.title: Administrator Validation
docs.text:
async def check_admin_permissions(self, interaction: discord.Interaction, *, defer: bool = False) -> bool:
    if defer and not interaction.response.is_done():
        await interaction.response.defer(ephemeral=True, thinking=True)
    bot_owner_id = getattr(self.tokens, 'bot_owner_id', 0)
    if interaction.user.guild_permissions.administrator or interaction.user.id == bot_owner_id:
        return True

    # Send localized permission denied message
    error_message = self.lang_manager.translate(...) or "您沒有權限執行此操作，僅限管理員使用此命令。"
    if interaction.response.is_done():
        await interaction.followup.send(error_message, ephemeral=True)
    else:
        await interaction.response.send_message(error_message, ephemeral=True)
    return False

docs.title: Bot Owner Override
docs.text:
• Bot owner has full administrative access regardless of server permissions
  • Configurable through addons.tokens.bot_owner_id
  • Provides emergency access for maintenance

docs.title: Mode Evaluation Flow
docs.text:
def is_allowed_channel(self, channel: discord.TextChannel, guild_id: str) -> Tuple[bool, bool, Optional[str]]:
    config = self.load_config(guild_id)
    channel_id = str(channel.id)
    auto_response_enabled = config.get("auto_response", {}).get(channel_id, False)

    # 1. Check channel-specific mode override
    channel_mode = config.get("channel_modes", {}).get(channel_id)
    if channel_mode:  # e.g., 'story'
        return True, auto_response_enabled, channel_mode

    # 2. Use server-wide mode
    server_mode = config.get("mode", "unrestricted")
    if server_mode == "unrestricted":
        return True, auto_response_enabled, server_mode
    elif server_mode == "whitelist":
        is_allowed = channel_id in config.get("whitelist", [])
        return is_allowed, auto_response_enabled, server_mode
    elif server_mode == "blacklist":
        is_allowed = channel_id not in config.get("blacklist", [])
        return is_allowed, auto_response_enabled, server_mode

    return False, False, server_mode

docs.title: Priority Order
docs.text:
1. Channel-specific modes (highest priority)
  2. Server-wide unrestricted mode
  3. Server-wide whitelist mode
  4. Server-wide blacklist mode
  5. Default deny (lowest priority)

docs.title: Configuration Sanitization
docs.text:
def _sanitize_schedule_data(self, data, fallback_channel_id: int):
    """Ensure config has required keys and proper types"""
    repaired = False

    if not isinstance(data, dict):
        data = {}
        repaired = True

    if 'mode' not in data:
        data['mode'] = "unrestricted"
        repaired = True

    # Ensure arrays exist
    for key in ['whitelist', 'blacklist']:
        if key not in data or not isinstance(data[key], list):
            data[key] = []
            repaired = True

    # Ensure dictionaries exist
    for key in ['auto_response', 'channel_modes']:
        if key not in data or not isinstance(data[key], dict):
            data[key] = {}
            repaired = True

    return data, repaired

docs.text:
• Atomic Writes: Temporary file + rename for safety
  • Error Handling: Graceful handling of file system errors
  • UTF-8 Encoding: Proper text encoding for internationalization

docs.title: Efficient Configuration Loading

docs.title: Memory Management
